title: Thelypteris japenensis
taxonomy: Thelypteris japenensis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh fern family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and evolutionary relationships. The genus Thelypteris is itself part of the Thelypteridaceae family, which contains over 1,000 species of ferns. Thelypteris japenensis specifically belongs to the Thelypteris genus and is one of the many species within it. It is also classified as a member of the order Polypodiales, which consists of various species of ferns.
uses: Thelypteris japenensis is known for its potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to help treat various health conditions.
